Recite as many as you can from memory. If you have not already memorized these poems or cannot recite them, thatis fine, just read and enjoy them.Reading 8 Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 4Read for about 1 hour.3/30/26, 9:11 AMPage 5 of 6

Go over any mistakes from Thursday’sNoun Review with your teacher.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 1Begin Lesson 10. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 10. Then look at the Lesson 10 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 26, Day: 2Lesson 24. Review all noun declensions (see charts in lesson XXIV). Read the twenty-fourth lesson. Oral Drill: conjugate “volo,”“nolo,” “malo,” and “fero.” Do the Day 1 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:volo, vis, vult, volumus, vultis, voluntnolo, non vis, non vult, nolumus, non vultis, noluntmalo, mavis, mavult, malumus, mavultis, maluntfero, fers, fert, ferimus, feritis, feruntHistory & Geog 8 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 1Start reading Adam of the Road by Elizabeth Gray (in print). This book is somewhat longer than the others you have been reading.We’ll plan to take three weeks to read it.Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 2Memorize.Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 4Read Unit 4, Chapter 15, Diego Velazquez.Reading 8 Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 5Read for about 1 hour.3/30/26, 9:11 AMPage 6 of 6

blocks.assignment_text: Begin Lesson 10. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 10. Then look at the Lesson 10 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.

blocks.subject: Beginning Latin III
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 24. Review all noun declensions (see charts in lesson XXIV). Read the twenty-fourth lesson. Oral Drill: conjugate "volo,""nolo," "malo," and "fero." Do the Day 1 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:volo, vis, vult, volumus, vultis, voluntnolo, non vis, non vult, nolumus, non vultis, noluntmalo, mavis, mavult, malumus, mavultis, maluntfero, fers, fert, ferimus, feritis, ferunt

blocks.subject: History & Geog 8
blocks.assignment_text: Start reading Adam of the Road by Elizabeth Gray (in print). This book is somewhat longer than the others you have been reading.We'll plan to take three weeks to read it.

blocks.subject: Poetry 8
blocks.assignment_text: Memorize.

blocks.subject: Art 7
blocks.assignment_text: Read Unit 4, Chapter 15, Diego Velazquez.

blocks.subject: Reading 8
blocks.assignment_text: Read for about 1 hour.

Weekly List for John Paul McAdams
Monday, March 30, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 67
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes reviewing any of the questions for this year that you find you need to work on. Also review the spiritual andcorporal works of mercy. Read Chapter 17 of Our Life in the Church.
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Do workbook pages 135-136. (T.E. pages 268-271.)
Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 32: find the errors and mark them in your book.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Look at the Familiar Words and Challenge Words for each root word in Lesson 9. If there are any you do not know, or can't figureout, look them up. You can add them to your notebook. Go over any of the Key Words you had trouble spelling.
Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS)Lesson 23. Do the Day 2 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.
Science 7 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 7 Year 1)Re-read Biology — Chapter 7: Section 7.2. Answer question 7.2b and The Big Idea.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Read The Magna Charta.
Poetry 8 SyllabusRecite and also try rendering the next 10 lines in prose.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2)Optional: Do Art Pad #14, Baroque Garland Project, Part 2. 
 
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
Tuesday, March 31, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 68
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes reviewing the seven capital sins and the four cardinal virtues. Also review the spiritual and corporal works of mercy.Discuss the counsels of perfection. What are they? What do they mean?
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Go over workbook pages 137 with your teacher. Then do workbook page 138. Do workbook page 139 with your teacher. (T.E. pages272-277.)Students may find this video on Appositives helpful: https://vimeo.com/1006216568/c08b51347e?share=copy
Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 32: write the selection correctly.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Do the Lesson 9 Exercises. Go over any Lesson 9 Key Words you had trouble spelling.
Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS)Lesson 23. Do the Day 3 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.
Science 7 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 7 Year 1)Read Biology — Chapter 7: Section 7.3. Answer question 7.3 in your workbook.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Finish The Magna Charta.
Poetry 8 SyllabusRecite and finish putting the selection in your own words.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
 
Wednesday, April 1, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Test 16
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes reviewing any of the questions for this year that you find you need to work on. Also review the spiritual andcorporal works of mercy. Read pp. 211-214 in your Baltimore Catechism. Discuss Chapter 17 of Our Life in the Church with yourteacher using the discussion questions in Chapter 17 Review in Our Life and the Church Quiz Book (see syllabus resources). Theexercises could be used for additional discussion or to encourage independent work. (Note: one could also use the questions inChapter 17 Lessons 1-4 of the activity book as a guide.)
Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 33: find the errors and mark them in your book.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Have someone read you the definition of the Lesson 9 Key Words and you write down the word. If you had trouble spelling any ofthem, practice.
Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS)Lesson 23. Take Quiz 23.
Science 7 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 7 Year 1)Read Biology — Chapter 7: Section 7.4. Answer question 7.4 and The Big Idea in your workbook.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Geography: Complete Chapter 4, Section 3 (Canada) in Eighth Grade Geography.OR If you have The Ultimate Geography and Timeline Guide: Turn to p. 163. Read this page and the next. Then, using a photocopyof the map on p. 234, identify the places listed in the ‘Map-It' section of p.164.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-1928 1-10)Listen to the Clarinet Quintet by Mozart.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
 
Thursday, April 2, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 69
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes reviewing any of the questions for this year that you find you need to work on. Discuss the following:A priest is a mediator between God and man. Christ is the one priest in Whom the fullness of the priesthood is found. Use ananalogy to show how other priests share in the priesthood of Christ. (p. 213)
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Do Noun Review, pages 140-143. (T.E. pages 278-285.)
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Take a spelling test on Lesson 9 Key words. If there are definitions you are unsure of, read them over again.
Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS)Attend Class. Do the Lesson XXIII Quia Games. 
Science 7 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 7 Year 1)Read Biology — Chapter 7: Section 7.5. Do the Chapter 7 Review in your workbook.
Poetry 8 SyllabusStart memorizing "The Merchant of Venice", Act V, Scene I, lines 53-88 (this passage begins with "How sweet the moonlight sleepsupon this bank..."). Spend five minutes each day this week working on the first 4 lines, from "How sweet the moonlight sleeps…" to"...sweet harmony."Review the poems you learned in Seventh Grade (see the Poetry List for Kindergarten to Eighth Grade found at the end of theselesson plans). Recite as many as you can from memory. If you have not already memorized these poems or cannot recite them, thatis fine, just read and enjoy them.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
 
Friday, April 3, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 70
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Study for the Noun Test by reviewing any parts of the noun section that you had trouble with. Go over any mistakes from Thursday'sNoun Review with your teacher.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Begin Lesson 10. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 10. Then look at the Lesson 10 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.
Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS)Lesson 24. Review all noun declensions (see charts in lesson XXIV). Read the twenty-fourth lesson. Oral Drill: conjugate "volo,""nolo," "malo," and "fero." Do the Day 1 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:volo, vis, vult, volumus, vultis, voluntnolo, non vis, non vult, nolumus, non vultis, noluntmalo, mavis, mavult, malumus, mavultis, maluntfero, fers, fert, ferimus, feritis, ferunt
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Start reading Adam of the Road by Elizabeth Gray (in print). This book is somewhat longer than the others you have been reading.We'll plan to take three weeks to read it.
Poetry 8 SyllabusMemorize.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2)Read Unit 4, Chapter 15, Diego Velazquez.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour.

Weekly List for Joseph McAdamsMonday, March 30, 2026Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 2Lesson 60Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles) Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 2Discuss the questions for Chapter 17. Or, if you prefer, you may write out your answers.1. Paul and his companions went to Thessalonica. What did they do there? And for how long?2. What happened and why?3. Next Paul went to Berea. How did the Jews there receive him?4. What happened at Berea? Where did Paul go next?5. Paul preached to the Athenians. What idea did he use to begin telling them about Jesus? Why did he use this instead of theLaw and the prophets of the Old Testament?Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards you worked on previously.English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 9, Day: 3Start today by going over workbook pages 73 and 75 with your teacher. Then do workbook pages 74 and 76. (T.E. pages 138-145.)Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 3Mini Review Lessons 4-6, Selection 30: find the errors and mark them in your book.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 1Write sentences using the Lesson 7 Key words. You can put each word in a separate sentence, but if you use more than one word inthe same sentence that is even better. Make sure your sentences are intelligible.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 21, Day: 3Lesson 19. Do the Day 2 exercises. Answer the questions in your workbook3/30/26, 1:13 PMPage 1 of 7

Also review three cards youworked on previously.English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 9, Day: 4Do workbook pages 77-79 today.  Wednesday, April 1, 2026Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 4Lesson 61Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles) Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 4Discuss the questions for Chapter 18, or write out your answers.1. blocks.subject: Math 87
blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 60

blocks.subject: Religion 7
blocks.assignment_text: Discuss the questions for Chapter 17. Or, if you prefer, you may write out your answers.1. Paul and his companions went to Thessalonica. What did they do there? And for how long?2. What happened and why?3. Next Paul went to Berea. How did the Jews there receive him?4. What happened at Berea? Where did Paul go next?5. Paul preached to the Athenians. What idea did he use to begin telling them about Jesus? Why did he use this instead of theLaw and the prophets of the Old Testament?Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards you worked on previously.

blocks.subject: English 8
blocks.assignment_text: Start today by going over workbook pages 73 and 75 with your teacher. Then do workbook pages 74 and 76. (T.E. pages 138-145.)

blocks.subject: Editing 7
blocks.assignment_text: Mini Review Lessons 4-6, Selection 30: find the errors and mark them in your book.

blocks.subject: Spelling & Vocabulary 8
blocks.assignment_text: Write sentences using the Lesson 7 Key words. You can put each word in a separate sentence, but if you use more than one word inthe same sentence that is even better. Make sure your sentences are intelligible.

blocks.assignment_text: Lesson 19. Do the Day 2 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.

blocks.assignment_text: Finish Archimedes and the Door of Science.
